channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Chadian military base attacked by Boko Haram, over 20 soldiers killed
item.description: Islamist militants from Boko Haram launched a deadly attack on a military base in Chad's Lake Chad region on Monday night, resulting in the deaths of at least 23 security personnel and injuring 26 oth...

item.title: Quiz: Identify the Country Between Ecuador and Bolivia
item.description: This article presents a travel quiz aimed at testing the knowledge of avid travellers about South American geography. Specifically, it challenges readers to identify which country lies between Ecuador...

item.title: How the 2026 Federal Budget Affects Generation X
item.description: As the Albanese government enters its second term, attention is turning to intergenerational equity, particularly how the upcoming federal budget will impact different age groups. Prime Minister Antho...

item.title: Six People Die, Eight Injured in Zamfara Road Bomb Blast
item.description: On Thursday, May 7, 2026, tragedy struck the Bagega–Anka road in Zamfara State when a Volkswagen Golf carrying passengers from Bagega to Anka hit an Improvised Explosive Device (IED). Six passengers d...

item.title: Infant dies following emergency response at home in Fallowfield, Manchester
item.description: A tragic event occurred on Thursday morning in Fallowfield, south Manchester, when a baby reportedly died following an incident at a private residence on Platt Lane. Emergency services, including two ...

item.title: Community mourns death of Métis elder Dory Cook as woman faces murder charge in Saskatoon
item.description: Family, friends, and community members in Saskatoon are mourning the death of Dory Cook, a 77-year-old Métis woman remembered for her compassion, advocacy, and cultural contributions. Cook was found d...

item.title: Rising ocean temperatures disrupt vital microbe–seagrass interactions in coastal ecosystems
item.description: Research conducted in Myuna Bay, on Lake Macquarie in New South Wales, offers fresh insight into how rising ocean temperatures may affect seagrass ecosystems. The site has experienced artificially ele...

item.title: Over 1,000 Protesters Rally in Durban Over Concerns About Illegal Immigration
item.description: On Wednesday, Durban witnessed a protest involving over 1,000 people organised by the anti-immigrant group March and March. The march commenced at King Dinuzulu Park and proceeded to Point Road, endin...

item.description: Following the announcement of the Tamil Nadu election results for 2026, actor-turned-politician Vijay has reached out to a new political party after facing a deadlock situation with the Governor over ...

item.title: Starmer under pressure as Labour support slips in Wales and local UK elections
item.description: The United Kingdom is heading into a series of local and regional elections that could significantly weaken Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s leadership. Polling suggests Labour may suffer substantial los...

item.title: Ian McKellen Reflects on Acting, Theatre, and Iconic Roles
item.description: In an extensive interview, Sir Ian McKellen reflects on his more than six decades in acting, discussing the evolution of theatre training and the enduring appeal of live performances. He reminisces ab...

item.title: Kenya Urged to Fast-Track Carbon Credit Policies to Support Climate Investments
item.description: Climate experts and stakeholders in Kenya are pushing the government to speed up regulations on carbon credit trading and methane financing, warning that slow policy approvals are holding back investm...

item.title: New Zealand Needs a Population Strategy with Realistic Goals and Inclusive Planning
item.description: Across advanced economies, demographic challenges are increasingly shaping public policy. Aotearoa New Zealand, despite decades of expert calls, still lacks a coherent national population strategy. Th...

item.title: Woman charged with terrorism offences after returning to Australia from Syria
item.description: A 32-year-old Australian woman, believed to be Janai Safar, has been charged with terrorism-related offences after arriving in Sydney from overseas. Australian Federal Police (AFP) officers removed he...

item.title: Man Arrested Near Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor's Home Following Intimidating Incident
item.description: A man has been apprehended on suspicion of a public order offence and possession of an offensive weapon close to the residence of Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor in Norfolk. Norfolk Police responded to rep...
